segments.name: formation_intro
segments.text: Offense lines up in a Stretch Bob formation from Over G alignment. This is a 2 wide receiver, 1 tight end set with both a running back and fullback in the backfield. Formation strength is to the left side, with the tight end split wide to the right and the fullback offset left. The play 'Stretch Bob' is an outside zone run designed to attack the right edge.

segments.name: snap
segments.text: The center delivers a clean snap to the quarterback to initiate the stretch run play to the right side.

segments.name: protection
segments.text: The offensive line executes a zone blocking scheme, with all five linemen working in unison to create horizontal movement and establish running lanes to the right side. The line slides right to seal the edge for the stretch run.

segments.name: te_route
segments.text: Tight end executes a reach block on the strong safety, working to seal the edge and create the outside running lane on the right side of the formation.

segments.name: fb_route
segments.text: Fullback leads the way as a lead blocker from the left side, working across the formation to target the Will linebacker and create a running lane for the tailback on the right edge.

segments.name: wr_z_route
segments.text: Wide receiver Z executes a stalk block on the right cornerback, maintaining outside leverage and preventing the defender from crashing down on the stretch run.

segments.name: wr_x_route
segments.text: Wide receiver X executes a crack block on the left cornerback, sealing the backside and preventing pursuit from the weak side of the formation.

segments.name: passing_primary
segments.text: The quarterback's primary option is to hand the ball to the running back on the stretch run to the right side.

segments.name: passing_checkdowns
segments.text: The quarterback executes the handoff, and the running back follows his blockers, reading the defensive flow to find the optimal running lane.

segments.name: rb_route
segments.text: Running back takes the handoff and presses the line of scrimmage to the right, reading the blocks and looking to bend the run outside or bang it inside based on defensive pursuit and lane development.
